sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
After the long start/finish straight, brake just before the 100m board and shift down to 4th gear, this left-hander has a wide entry but a tightening apex. Keep it clean and get on the throttle early — youll need speed for the short straight ahead.

These are taken at high speed — lift slightly if needed, and keep the steering smooth. Turn 2 is flat in qualifying trim, while Turn 3 may require a slight lift, depending on grip. Stick tight to the racing line through Turn 4 to set up the heavy braking zone that follows.

A tight left-hander who sets up the first DRS straight. Brake hard at around 100m, shift down to 2nd gear, and rotate the car quickly. Avoid aggressive curb usage as it can destabilise your exit.

Formerly part of a chicane, this is now a single wide-apex hairpin. Use a late apex in 2nd gear, focusing entirely on exit speed — this leads onto the longest straight of the circuit and the first DRS zone. Shift up early to minimise wheelspin.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
At the end of the back straight, this is one of the biggest overtaking zones. Brake at the 100m board, and drop to 2nd gear. Aim for a clean apex and a quick exit. The kerbs here can be harsh — be cautious.

A gentle left kink, flat-out and quick. Prepare the car for the next major braking zone. Manage ERS deployment wisely here — this is a good place to harvest or attack.

New for recent years, this long, banked left-hander replaces the former slow chicane. Brake early, stay in 3rd gear, and balance the throttle through the corner. Let the banking help rotate the car. Dont overdrive; its tempting, but it will only hurt exit speed.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
This is a beautifully flowing right-hand sequence. Stay in 4th or 5th gear, keep tight to the inside, and let the car naturally drift outward. A smooth steering rhythm here pays dividends. This section is all about maintaining speed while managing tyre wear.

Brake hard just before the apex and drop to 3rd gear. This is a short, sharp corner — try not to overslow the car. It leads into a series of technical corners.

Stay tight through these last turns under the iconic Yas Hotel lights. Brake into Turn 14, a slow left, hold 2nd or 3rd gear, and be patient. Turn 15 immediately follows with a quick change of direction. Then swing right for Turn 16 — a key corner to exit smoothly onto the final straight. Good throttle control is crucial for achieving a good end to the lap.
